back_translation: And Samuel died, and all Israel gathered and mourned for him, and they buried him in his house at Ramah. And David arose and went down to the wilderness of Paran.
And there was a man in Maon, whose business was in Carmel, and the man was very great, and he had three thousand sheep and a thousand goats, and it happened that he was shearing his sheep in Carmel.
And the name of the man was Nabal, and the name of his wife was Abigail. And the woman was good of intelligence and beautiful of form, but the man was harsh and evil of deeds, and he was a Calebite.
And David heard in the wilderness that Nabal was shearing his sheep.
And David sent ten young men, and David said to the young men, 'Go up to Carmel, and go to Nabal, and ask him in my name for peace.
And you shall say thus, To life! And you peace, and your house peace, and all that is yours peace.
And now I have heard that there are shearers for you. Now your shepherds who were with us — we did not put them to shame, and nothing was missing for them all the days they were in Carmel.

Ask your young men, and they will tell you. And let the young men find favor in your eyes, for on a good day we have come. Give please what your hand will find to your servants and to your son to David.'
And the young men of David came and spoke to Nabal according to all these words in the name of David, and they waited.
And Nabal answered the servants of David, and said, 'Who is David, and who is the son of Jesse? Today there are many servants who break away each man from before his master.
And shall I take my bread and my water and my meat that I have slaughtered for my shearers, and give to men whom I do not know from where they are?'
And the young men of David turned to their way and returned and came and told him all these words.

And David said to his men, 'Strap on, each man his sword.' And each man strapped on his sword, and David also strapped on his sword, and about four hundred men went up after David, and two hundred stayed with the equipment.
And to Abigail, the wife of Nabal, one of the young men from the young men told, saying, 'Behold, David sent messengers from the wilderness to bless our master, but he flew at them.
And the men were very good to us, and we were not put to shame, and we missed nothing all the days we walked with them when we were in the field.
A wall they were upon us, both by night and by day, all the days we were with them shepherding the sheep.
And now, know and see what you will do, for evil is determined against our master and against all his house. And he is a son of Belial — from speaking to him.'

And Abigail hurried and took two hundred loaves and two skins of wine, and five sheep prepared, and five seahs of roasted grain, and a hundred clusters of raisins, and two hundred cakes of figs, and placed them on donkeys.
And she said to her young men, 'Pass on before me, behold I am coming after you.' But to her husband Nabal she did not tell.
And it was, as she was riding on the donkey and going down by the cover of the mountain, behold, David and his men were coming down to meet her, and she met them.
And David had said, 'Surely in vain I guarded all that belonged to this one in the wilderness, and nothing was missing of all that was his — but he has returned to me evil instead of good.
May God do thus to the enemies of David and thus may He add, if I leave of all that is his until morning a male.'
And Abigail saw David, and hurried and got down from on the donkey, and fell before David on her face and bowed to the ground.
And she fell at his feet and said, 'On me, my lord, be the iniquity. And please let your servant speak in your ears, and hear the words of your servant.

Let not my lord set his heart please against this man of Belial, against Nabal, for as his name is, so is he — Nabal is his name, and folly is with him. And I your servant did not see the young men of my lord whom you sent.

And now, my lord, as the LORD lives and as your soul lives — that the LORD has held you back from entering into bloodshed, and from saving yourself with your own hand — and now, may your enemies be like Nabal, and those who seek evil against my lord.
And now, this blessing that your servant has brought to my lord — let it be given to the young men who walk at the feet of my lord.

Forgive please the transgression of your servant, for the LORD will surely make for my lord a faithful house, because my lord fights the LORD's battles, and no evil shall be found in you from your days.

And should a man arise to pursue you and to seek your soul, then the soul of my lord will be bound in the bundle of the living with the LORD your God, and the soul of your enemies — He will sling it in the midst of the hollow of the sling.
And it will be when the LORD does to my lord according to all the good that He has spoken concerning you, and appoints you ruler over Israel,

and this will not be to you for a staggering and for a stumbling of heart to my lord — to shed blood without cause and to save my lord for himself. And when the LORD deals well with my lord, remember your servant.'
And David said to Abigail, 'Blessed be the LORD God of Israel, who sent you this day to meet me.
And blessed be your discernment, and blessed are you, who restrained me this day from entering into bloodshed and from saving myself with my own hand.

And however, as the LORD God of Israel lives, who has held me back from doing evil to you — for unless you had hurried and come to meet me, surely there would not have been left to Nabal until the light of morning a male.'
And David took from her hand what she had brought to him, and to her he said, 'Go up to your house in peace. See, I have heard your voice and have lifted up your face.'

And Abigail came to Nabal, and behold, he had a feast in his house like a feast of the king, and Nabal's heart was good upon him, and he was drunk to a great extent. And she did not tell him anything small or great until the light of morning.
And it happened in the morning, when the wine had gone out from Nabal, that his wife told him these things, and his heart died within him, and he became like a stone.
And it happened about ten days later, that the LORD struck Nabal and he died.

And David heard that Nabal was dead, and he said, 'Blessed be the LORD, who has pleaded the cause of my reproach from the hand of Nabal, and has held back His servant from evil — and the evil of Nabal the LORD has returned upon his head.' And David sent and spoke to Abigail to take her to him as a wife.
And the servants of David came to Abigail at Carmel, and they spoke to her, saying, 'David sent us to you to take you to him as a wife.'
And she arose and bowed face to the ground, and said, 'Behold, your servant is a maidservant, to wash the feet of the servants of my lord.'
And Abigail hurried and arose, and rode on the donkey, with five of her maidens going at her feet, and she walked after the messengers of David, and became his wife.
And David had also taken Ahinoam from Jezreel, and both of them were his wives.
And Saul gave his daughter Michal, David's wife, to Palti son of Laish, who was from Gallim.
